commit.message:
feat: optimize CI test suite for speed (#1067) (#1077)

- Add xunit.runner.json to all 6 test projects with parallelism config
  - Data/Host tests: parallelizeTestCollections=false (SharedState collections)
  - Other tests: parallelizeTestCollections=true for faster execution

- Parallelize L1.4 (large perf) with L1.3 (small perf) in ci-cd-pipeline.yml
- Disable analyzers during test builds in Directory.Build.props
- L1.5 Playwright now waits for L1.2 + L1.3 + L1.4 (all parallel perf jobs)

commit.message:
feat(messaging): replace 5s poll loop with Redis pub/sub push delivery

- delivery.rs: after XADD to user Redis stream, PUBLISH to inbox:wakeup:{user_id}
  (fire-and-forget — non-critical, poll loop is the fallback)
- messaging-service: add spawn_inbox_wakeup() — subscribes to inbox:wakeup:{uid}
  channel via dedicated Redis pub/sub connection, forwards signals via mpsc
- MessageStream loop: adds wakeup arm to tokio::select! for immediate delivery
  when a message arrives; subscribes lazily if user_id not known at stream open
- Fallback poll interval: 5s → 60s (handles reconnects / missed pub/sub events)

Before: receipts and messages delivered with up to 5s latency; N streams = N
DB queries every 5 seconds. After: near-instant delivery via pub/sub push;
poll only fires as safety net every 60s.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Auto-add GDAL virtual filesystem prefixes for URLs, cloud storage, and archives (#144)

Adds a data-driven _gdal_path() function that automatically prepends
the appropriate /vsi*/ prefix:

- Cloud storage schemes (s3://, gs://, az://, oss://, swift://) are
  stripped and replaced with /vsis3/, /vsigs/, /vsiaz/, etc.
- Network URLs (http://, https://, ftp://) are prepended with /vsicurl/.
- Archive extensions (.zip, .gz, .tar, .tar.gz, .tgz, .7z, .rar, .kmz,
  .ods, .xlsx, .lpk(x), .mpk(x), .ppkx) are wrapped with /vsizip/,
  /vsigzip/, /vsitar/, /vsi7z/, or /vsirar/. Extensions are detected
  at path boundaries to support subpaths (archive.zip/dir/file.shp).
- Prefixes chain when both apply, e.g.
  https://host/data.zip -> /vsizip//vsicurl/https://host/data.zip
- Existing /vsi* prefixes are left untouched.
- An @info message is logged when a path is rewritten.

The top-level read() also applies _gdal_path and forces ArchGDALDriver
for rewritten paths, since native drivers cannot handle /vsi* paths.

Adding new handlers requires only adding an entry to the lookup tables.

Fixes #143, fixes #122

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add brace escape sequences \{ and \} to lexer (#546)

The converter emits \{ and \} in strings to escape literal braces
(from C# string.Format patterns like "{0}"). Without lexer support,
these produce Calor0004 "Invalid escape sequence" errors.

Fixes 7 of 13 compile failures in latest Newtonsoft.Json conversion.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add GPS valid flag, battery/MPU status, millis timestamp for hardware integration

Arduino firmware:
- Add explicit gv (GPS valid) flag so server doesnt rely on lat/lng==0 check
- Add bat field (100 placeholder - no battery sensor)
- Add mpu field (MPU6050 availability status)
- Add ms field (millis() uptime for timing/gap detection)
- Increase StaticJsonDocument to 300 bytes for new fields

Server bridge:
- Use gv flag for GPS validity instead of lat/lng==0 (fixes equator edge case)
- Check mpu field for systemStatus (offline if both GPS+MPU down, warning if either)

fix: Quiz component handles string props for RSC compatibility

next-mdx-remote@6 RSC mode silently drops JSX expression props
(arrays like options={[...]} and numbers like answer={1}).

Quiz now accepts pipe-separated strings: options="A|B|C" answer="1"
and parses them at runtime. Falls back gracefully if props are missing.

commit.message:
fix(blazor): downgrade SignalR.Client 10.0.3 -> 9.0.3 (fixes circuit crash)

ROOT CAUSE: Microsoft.AspNetCore.SignalR.Client 10.0.3 (.NET 10) was
referenced in a .NET 9 project. The .NET 10 MessagePackHubProtocolWorker
calls IInvocationBinder.GetTarget(ReadOnlySpan<byte>) which does not
exist in .NET 9's Blazor runtime, causing MissingMethodException wrapped
as InvalidDataException: Reading 'target' as String failed.

Also downgraded StackExchangeRedis 10.0.3 -> 9.0.3 for version consistency.

Verified with Playwright browser: circuit connects, buttons work, tabs
open/close, real-time data updates, navigation works. No console errors.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use pipe-separated strings for Quiz options in MDX

next-mdx-remote@6 RSC mode drops JSX expression props (arrays/numbers).
Changed options={["A","B"]} to options="A|B" and answer={1} to answer="1".

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(docker): load quicklisp setup.lisp explicitly instead of add-to-init-file

(ql:add-to-init-file) fails in the Docker build because the init file
path returns NIL in a fresh root environment. Replace it by explicitly
passing --load /root/quicklisp/setup.lisp in the pre-fetch RUN step
and in the CMD, removing the dependency on .sbclrc entirely.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix duplicate client/operation names when operation IDs contain multiple underscores (#5348)

* Initial plan

* Fix duplicate class/method generation when operation IDs contain multiple underscores

Use the first underscore as the separator in MultipleClientsFromOperationIdOperationNameGenerator:
- GetClientName: everything before the first '_' = client name
- GetOperationName: everything after the first '_' = operation name

This guarantees uniqueness since OpenAPI requires globally unique operation IDs.
Previously, 'Orders_items_get' and 'Products_items_get' both produced
client='items', operation='get', causing duplicate generated code.

Co-authored-by: lahma <171892+lahma@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: lahma <171892+lahma@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: export writer crash and notification badge persistence

- Fix always-true destinations.length >= 0 guard in ExportWriterView
- Clear stale destinations on tripId change to prevent invalid API calls
- Add trip?.id null safety in WritingAssistantPanel session creation
- Wrap ExportWriterView in ErrorBoundary for graceful failure
- Optimistically reset unreadCount when notification panel opens
- Await markAllAsRead and add error handling with optimistic update
- Pause unread count polling while notification panel is open

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: measure ping via TCP connect instead of full RPC round-trip

Previously ping_ms timed the entire health_check() call (HTTP connect
+ JSON encode + server processing + response decode), inflating the
displayed latency. Now a raw TCP connect to the RPC port is used for
the ping measurement, and health_check() is called separately only if
the TCP probe succeeds. This gives true network round-trip time.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: thread CancellationToken through ProjectMigrator and preserve XML doc comments (#545)

- Thread CancellationToken from MCP tools through ProjectMigrator.ExecuteAsync
  to enable proper cancellation of batch conversions (root cause of Newtonsoft
  memory exhaustion - server kept converting after client timeout)
- Return partial results on cancellation instead of throwing away progress
- Link caller's cancellation token with per-file timeout via CreateLinkedTokenSource
- Preserve XML doc comments during C# to Calor conversion as line comments
- Add DocComment property to AstNode base class
- Extract summary, @param, @returns, @remarks, @throws, @typeparam from trivia
- Emit doc comments above classes, interfaces, enums, methods, properties, constructors

commit.message:
Agent: Target State Alignment (#7547)

* Agent: Target State Alignment

* Initial plan

* Migrate dominantLineEnding from lint/refactor to @gml-modules/core

Boundary violation: `dominantLineEnding` was defined separately in
`lint/rule-helpers.ts` and `refactor/.../loop-length-hoisting-codemod.ts`
with divergent implementations. Per target-state.md §2.1, shared text
utilities belong in `@gml-modules/core`.

- Add `dominantLineEnding(text: string): "\r\n" | "\n"` to
  `src/core/src/utils/line-breaks.ts` with the correct count-based
  implementation (CRLF count vs bare-LF count; tie defaults to LF).
- Remove the local definition from `lint/rule-helpers.ts`.
- Update 8 lint rules that imported it to use `Core.dominantLineEnding`.
- Update the refactor loop-length-hoisting codemod to use the shared
  Core implementation (also corrects behaviour: was existence-based,
  now count-based, matching the rest of the codebase).
- Add 6 unit tests in `src/core/test/line-breaks.test.ts`.
- Add `dominant-line-ending-boundary.test.ts` in the lint workspace to
  catch future regression (verifies Core exports the function and
  rule-helpers.ts does not redefine it).

Co-authored-by: henrylkirk <15679826+henrylkirk@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

Initial release — Innerflect v1.0

Privacy-first AI companion running 100% in your browser.
No conversation data ever leaves your device.

- WebLLM/WebGPU in-browser AI (6 model tiers, auto-detected)

- Freemium session limits (anon 30min, free 60min, Pro unlimited)

- Chat history for Pro users
- Landing page, FAQ, Privacy policy
- Mobile responsive, cross-browser support screen
- GhostSlayer ad engine (Landing page only)
- FastAPI backend + PostgreSQL + Redis
- Caddy reverse proxy + ngrok/Tailscale tunnel support

GPU: TFLOPS system replaces slot-based capacity

- GPUs now generate TFLOPS (5/15/35/100 per level), a real-time compute resource
- Each AI consumes TFLOPS (4/8/18/54 per level) to function
- TFLOPS is not stored  it's a live bandwidth check
- GPU no longer has ongoing \$ drain (elCost: 0), one-time HC purchase only
- Stats panel shows TFLOPS: generated / required with color coding
- AI planner updated for TFLOPS-based heuristics
- README updated with TFLOPS tables

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: harden non-destructive guards and config validation (#38) (#42)

- Expand BLOCKED_SUBCOMMANDS with 14 additional mutating commands:
  switch, restore, cherry-pick, revert, pull, apply, am, init,
  clone, gc, prune, bisect, filter-branch, update-ref

- Expand BLOCKED_ARG_SEQUENCES with 25 additional destructive
  sequences for mixed-mode commands: branch rename/copy/delete,
  remote add/remove/rename/set-url, worktree add/remove/prune,
  submodule add/init/update/deinit, notes add/remove/edit/merge/prune,
  reflog delete/expire

- Add .strict() to all Zod config schemas to reject unknown properties
  matching schema.json additionalProperties:false intent

- Enforce allowExtraArgs/extraArgsSchema consistency:
  allowExtraArgs=true requires extraArgsSchema,
  allowExtraArgs=false forbids extraArgsSchema

- Validate default values are compatible with enum constraints
  at config load time

- Add 80 new unit tests covering all additions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
security: add helmet, rate limiting, input validation, CORS config

- Added helmet.js for security headers (X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, etc.)
- Added express-rate-limit: 100 req/15min global, 10 req/15min on auth endpoints
- Added CORS configuration with credentials support and preflight caching
- Added request body size limit (1MB)
- Created validate.middleware.js with input validators for:
  - Report creation (lat/lng bounds, waterLevel enum, URL validation)
  - Nearby reports query (lat/lng/radius bounds)
  - Emergency creation (lat/lng, priority enum, description sanitization)
  - ID parameter validation
- Created role.middleware.js for role-based access control (RBAC)
- XSS prevention: strip angle brackets + length limits on text fields
- All 9 unit tests + 7 Flutter tests pass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix content-level citation accuracy: 26 line numbers + 1 default value

Flywheel rotation 6: Content-level citation verification
Checked 292 (identifier, file:line) claims — verified actual GAMS file
content matches the cited line number.

Fixes:
- 24 wrong line numbers across 9 module docs (mostly off by 1-5 lines
  due to code evolution since docs were written)
- 1 wrong variable prefix in module_20.md (i20_ vs f20_ at input.gms)
- 1 wrong default value in module_56.md (s56_cprice_red_factor default
  is 1, not 0)
- 1 citation line fix in module_32.md (s32_aff_prot location)

Also excluded 2 false positives (conceptually correct references where
the identifier name doesn't literally appear at the cited line).

Result: 266/292 content-verified claims (91%) → 290/292 (99.3%)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
